_instructions: Copy this file to <YOUR_PATTERN_ID>.json. Delete this _instructions field. Fill every field. Status must be DRAFT until G2 passes.
name: Short human-readable name for the failure pattern
trigger_shapes: Code shape 1 that should trigger checking this pattern
Code shape 2 — plain English, specific enough for a code reviewer to recognize
activation: The specific precipitating condition — what state change crosses from safe to failing? Minimum 50 chars.
mechanism: Step-by-step causal chain. Step 1 causes step 2 causes step 3. Each step must be causally connected to the next. Minimum 100 chars.

signal_sequence.signal: What monitoring sees (or doesn't see) at this phase. Specific enough to reference a real metric or log line.
The first observable deviation from normal. Which metric, which log, which threshold?
How the anomaly spreads or intensifies. What else starts failing?
What users or downstream systems experience. Be specific about the error or degradation.

threshold_estimated: Quantified estimate of the load, rate, or concurrency at which this pattern activates. Include the reasoning, not just the number.
invisible_because: The specific assumption or monitoring gap that causes this to look healthy when it isn't. Minimum 50 chars.
diagnostic: The specific question or query an on-call engineer runs to confirm or rule out this pattern. Specific enough to execute.
countermeasures: Countermeasure 1 — specific, named, actionable. Include technology or policy name.
Countermeasure 2 — cheaper or complementary to countermeasure 1.
Countermeasure 3 — at least one must be a test that would catch the failure.
